content.body: A question about Annex B. It's in informative section, but is referenced from the normative section 6, e.g. 6.2.1.3 "Where the product does not map to a defined use case, a risk assessment performed under the methodology of Annex B."  That seems to require the exact use of Annex B for a risk assessment. Is that the intent?
yeah, with regard to 6.2 we are pushing the envelope of appropriateness. I am reviewing this with Laure and will report on the matter on Monday.

Roger, thanks. I noted there's a must in there, too.  The reason for the question is that somebody flagged that we've got some internal risk assessment methodologies (you can imagine the security bureaucracy in a company our size) but that differ from Annex B, and whether using those would suffice.

I also thought that was a bit odd and I remember asking about it a few meetings back (if i remember correctly it was friday 27.06) and DanT explained (correct me if i'm wrong) that the commission specifically asked to include that requirement

* I also thought that was a bit odd and I remember asking about it a few meetings back (if i remember correctly it was friday 27.06) and Daniel explained (correct me if i'm wrong) that the commission specifically asked to include that requirement

content.body: Anyways, unrelated, I wanted to take a look at prENs 40000 this morning but they are behind paywalls...however, I remember we looked at a prEN 40000 a few weeks back and I was wondering if maybe we have access to them? through ETSI? does anyone know? thanks
